title: Alien: Romulus Now Franchise's Second Highest-Grossing Movie Ever At The Worldwide Box Office
text: The science fiction horror film Alien: Romulus has achieved significant box office success, becoming the second-highest grossing installment in the Alien franchise. Directed by Fede Álvarez and featuring Cailee Spaeny, the movie earned approximately $283.5 million worldwide by its third weekend, surpassing Alien: Covenant's $240.9 million global total. Only Prometheus (2012) has generated more revenue at $403.3 million. The article notes that to overtake Prometheus, Romulus would need an additional $119.8 million. Despite trailing in raw numbers, Romulus demonstrates superior financial efficiency, with a production budget of $80 million compared to Prometheus's $130 million expenditure. The film also maintains stronger critical reception, holding an 80% Rotten Tomatoes score versus Prometheus's 73%, both certified fresh ratings. The film's success is attributed to strong international performance, particularly in China, effective marketing strategies, and positive audience reception demonstrated by its B+ CinemaScore.
